meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Scaling SaaS Pricing Models Effectively
content.seo_description: Stop trading hours for dollars. Learn how to architect scalable SaaS pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code to break your project ceiling today.
content.answer_block: To scale beyond custom hourly work, you must transition to productized SaaS pricing. By leveraging Antigravity for automated boilerplate and Claude Code for iterative logic, you can deploy subscription-based tiers that decouple your income from your time, allowing you to serve hundreds of clients with the same codebase.
content.intro:
You’re stuck in the 'Freelancer’s Trap.' Every new client is a fresh sprint, every project is a mountain of custom code, and your revenue hits a hard ceiling defined by the hours in your day. You aren't building a business; you're building a job that owns you.

But the architecture of scale is simpler than you think. By shifting to a SaaS-first pricing model—where features are gated by tiered subscriptions—you transform bespoke services into repeatable, high-margin products. We’re going to use modern AI orchestration tools to build that infrastructure in hours, not months.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, map your features to user personas. Identify the 'Core' functionality for entry-level and 'Enterprise' triggers for upsells.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Basic, Pro, and Enterprise, including feature flags for each tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a TypeScript interface for the PricingTier schema defined in antigravity.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Automate billing using Stripe's API. This ensures your code doesn't just work; it gets paid while you sleep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout session handler in Node.js that maps the defined pricing tiers to Stripe Price IDs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the existing server logic to integrate the Stripe checkout handler.'

steps.title: Architect Middleware Gating
steps.description: Build a robust middleware layer that checks subscription status before allowing access to premium endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Express.js middleware function that verifies user session subscription status against the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Inject the subscription middleware into all routes under /api/v1/premium/'

steps.title: Automate Database Provisioning
steps.description: Ensure your database schema supports multi-tenancy so one codebase can serve thousands of distinct customers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema with a multi-tenant architecture including a 'subscription_status' field on the User model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the new Prisma schema and run migrations.'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Ship your infrastructure to production and verify that tier-gating triggers correctly for non-paying users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD deployment script for a SaaS boilerplate that runs feature tests on every commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute deployment pipeline and verify endpoint access for a free-tier user.'

pro_tips: Always use a 'grandfathering' flag in your database to keep early adopters happy without breaking your new pricing strategy.
Implement 'Usage-Based' thresholds rather than just feature flags to encourage users to upgrade naturally as they grow.
Use Claude Code to write your integration tests first; if the pricing logic doesn't pass the test, the feature shouldn't exist.
Avoid building your own authentication; use Clerk or Auth0 to handle the identity layer so you can focus on the business logic.
Set up Slack webhooks for every successful Stripe subscription event to get that 'dopamine hit' that keeps you motivated to scale.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing tiers are live, conduct an A/B test on your landing page pricing table to see which tier converts the most leads.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for clients and start building for the market. By automating your SaaS infrastructure, you unlock the ability to scale your revenue infinitely. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
